title: Deal Card v2 repair and finish (shared operator correctness)

workstreams.name: Canonical acreage and spatial basis
workstreams.operatorOutcome: Every Deal Card reconciles assessed/deeded/surveyed/GIS/provider/operator-accepted/valuation/overlay acreage with source, confidence, dispute and permitted-use metadata; the UI explains assessed-vs-mapped differences; overlays use the geometry actually queried; valuation discloses its basis; a material unresolved basis raises a reconciliation issue and Tyler decision; no gated calc silently uses an unresolved acreage.
inScope: Shared acreage-basis record model with the 8 basis kinds and 7 per-use attributes
Reconciliation of assessed vs mapped/geometry acreage into a discrepancy issue
Overlay (flood/wetlands/soils/slope/non-wetland) acreage sourced from queried geometry
Valuation acreage-basis disclosure
Consistency check: overlay area may not exceed queried geometry without documented explanation
Preserve accepted acreage; do not change without Tyler confirmation
workstreams.requiredDataState: A verified Deal Card whose assessed acreage differs from mapped/GIS geometry acreage (acceptance example: 200 Sid Edens Rd, Pickens SC, APN 5105-00-44-0497 — assessed 1.32 vs mapped 1.15).

steps: Open the verified Deal Card at http://localhost:3141
Read the acreage header on Overview and confirm it distinguishes assessed vs mapped and explains the difference
Open Due Diligence and confirm each overlay states which acreage basis it used and that overlay area does not exceed queried geometry
Confirm the valuation states the acreage basis it used
Confirm a material unresolved acreage basis surfaces a reconciliation issue and a Tyler decision item
Refresh the page and confirm all acreage statements persist

testRequirements: Unit tests for the acreage-basis record: each basis kind carries value/source/confidence/disputed/accepted/permittedUses/limitation
Reconciliation test: assessed!=geometry beyond tolerance yields a discrepancy issue and Tyler-decision flag
Overlay-vs-geometry consistency test fails when overlay area exceeds queried geometry without documented explanation
Regression: accepted acreage is never silently replaced
failureConditions: Header shows a single acreage without explaining assessed-vs-mapped difference
An overlay reports an acreage basis it did not query, or area exceeds queried geometry with no explanation
Valuation does not disclose its acreage basis
Material acreage discrepancy with no reconciliation issue or Tyler decision
A gated calculation uses an acreage the header treats as unresolved
An accepted acreage is changed without Tyler confirmation

requirements.text: The UI explains why assessed and mapped acreage differ.
Overlay acreage uses the actual geometry queried.
Valuation discloses exactly which acreage basis it uses.
A material unresolved acreage basis triggers a reconciliation issue and Tyler decision when appropriate.
No calculation silently uses an acreage the header treats as unresolved.
Flood, wetlands, soils, slope, non-wetland area, comps, valuation, maps, reports, and strategy math identify the correct acreage basis.
A consistency check fails when an overlay area exceeds its queried geometry without a documented explanation.
An accepted acreage is not changed without Tyler's confirmation.

workstreams.name: Research completeness and evidence language
workstreams.operatorOutcome: Research lane counts and wording separate provider-attempted, provider-retrieved, partial evidence, business-question-resolved, legal-confirmation-complete, and external-confirmation-required; partial proximity is not completed access research; a county flood query is not complete FEMA research; FEMA/road/soils/utilities/red-flag copy is correctly qualified.
inScope: Research-completeness state model separating the six evidence tiers per lane
FEMA lane: separate Zone X result, coverage basis, panel status, effective date, BFE availability; county-layer vs federal FIRM
Road/access lane: separate classification, parcel contact, ROW contact, physical access, driveway, legal access, maintenance; unknown vs private ownership
Soils/septic lane: single-component vs mixed ratings; SSURGO map-unit limitation vs site septic feasibility; map-unit slope vs measured mean slope
Utilities lane: absence of mapped line is not proof of unavailability; preliminary likelihood labels
Critical red flags: incomplete screening not shown favorable; no-all-clear language matches unresolved categories
workstreams.requiredDataState: A verified Deal Card with unresolved zoning, researching parcel-road contact, unresolved legal access, unreviewed deed/easement, pending FEMA panel (acceptance example: 200 Sid Edens Rd).

steps: Open the verified Deal Card and read the research-status summary
Confirm the evidenced-lane count does not count zoning, legal access, deed/easement review, or FEMA panel as complete when they are not
Open FEMA detail and confirm Zone X / coverage basis / panel status / effective date / BFE availability are separate and county-layer vs FIRM is distinguished
Open road/access detail and confirm proximity is not called frontage and unknown ownership is not called private
Open soils/septic and utilities detail and confirm qualified wording
Refresh and confirm wording and counts persist

testRequirements: Lane-completeness tests for each of the six evidence tiers
FEMA: BFE-screened claim requires retrieved BFE; exact-acreage+BFE label requires both supported
Access: partial proximity does not mark access research complete; unknown ownership not rendered as private
Soils: single mapped component not described as split/mixed; utilities absence not rendered as unavailable
Red flags: incomplete screening lowers critical-risk completeness
failureConditions: Evidenced-lane count includes lanes that have not genuinely resolved
BFE screened stated with no BFE retrieved, or exact-acreage+BFE marked complete unsupported
Road proximity shown as frontage, or unknown ownership shown as private
Single soil component described as split/mixed, or utility absence shown as unavailable
Incomplete screening presented favorably or no-all-clear language contradicts unresolved categories

requirements.text: Research counts separate provider-attempted, provider-retrieved, partial, business-resolved, legal-complete, external-required.
Partial proximity evidence is not counted as completed access research.
A county flood query is not counted as complete FEMA research when material FEMA tasks remain.

FEMA Zone X result, coverage basis, panel status, effective date, and BFE availability are separate; no unsupported BFE claim; county-layer vs FIRM distinguished; overlays use mapped geometry acreage.

Road: proximity is not frontage; unknown ownership is not called private; classification/contact/ROW/physical/driveway/legal/maintenance are separated; no unsupported recorded-private-road-rights claim.
Soils: single component not described as split/mixed; SSURGO limitation vs site septic feasibility distinguished; map-unit slope vs measured mean slope separated.
Utilities: absent mapped line is not proof of unavailability; well/septic labeled preliminary; provider research vs remaining confirmation separated.
Critical red flags: incomplete screening is not favorable; access/title/acreage/zoning uncertainty affects critical-risk completeness; no-all-clear language matches unresolved categories.

workstreams.name: Unified readiness and strategy status
workstreams.operatorOutcome: One shared readiness record drives Overview, Market, Strategy, Seller, Reports, RAG, and Executive review; strategy readiness cannot read OK while all strategies are blocked; value readiness is not high merely because a median exists; offer readiness explains why it is researching/blocked; the consistency audit fails on any status disagreement.
inScope: Shared readiness record separating research completeness, preliminary vs defensible valuation readiness, strategy screening availability vs scoreability vs actionability, offer readiness, contract readiness
All Deal Card tabs and reports consume the same readiness result
Materiality wiring: zoning/acreage-basis/access/title/physical constraints affect confidence and readiness
Consistency audit fails when visible statuses disagree
workstreams.requiredDataState: A verified Deal Card where all five strategies are blocked and offer readiness is researching (acceptance example: 200 Sid Edens Rd).

steps: Open the verified Deal Card Overview and read Strategy/Value/Offer readiness
Confirm Strategy Readiness is not OK/actionable while all five strategies are blocked
Open the Strategy tab and confirm screening-available vs actionability-blocked is expressed coherently
Confirm Value Readiness is not high merely because a median can be computed
Confirm Offer Readiness explains why it is researching/blocked
Open Market, Seller, Reports and confirm they show the same readiness result
Refresh and confirm readiness persists

testRequirements: All-strategies-blocked yields non-OK strategy readiness
Median-available alone does not yield high value readiness
Materiality: unresolved zoning/acreage/access/title lowers confidence and readiness
Single readiness record is the source for every tab/report
Audit fails on a seeded status disagreement
failureConditions: Strategy Readiness OK while all strategies blocked
Value Readiness high solely from a computable median
Offer Readiness with no explanation
Two tabs/reports showing different readiness for the same card
Consistency audit passes despite disagreeing statuses

requirements.text: If all five strategies are blocked, Strategy Readiness cannot display OK or actionable.
The UI may say strategy screening is available while actionability is blocked.
Value Readiness cannot be high or fully ready merely because a median can be calculated.
Zoning, acreage basis, access, title, and physical constraints affect confidence and readiness per materiality.
Offer readiness clearly explains why it remains researching or blocked.
All tabs and reports consume the same readiness result.
The consistency audit fails when visible statuses disagree.

workstreams.name: Comparable validation, provider coverage, and valuation
workstreams.operatorOutcome: Provider missions show real attempt/result and candidate/accepted/duplicate/rejected counts; every selected comp displays the full validation field set with a working or explicitly-missing provider link; weak/improved comps are penalized or excluded; materially different clusters are not blended into a high-confidence median; valuation discloses cluster, counts, median/weighted/average PPA, IQR, outliers, limitations, and confidence rationale; sold/valuation/map/registry counts reconcile.
inScope: Per-provider mission transparency (LandPortal, Zillow, Redfin, Realie.ai, Realtor/HomeHarvest, county) with attempt/result/blocker and candidate/accepted/duplicate/rejected counts
Selected-comp validation record with the full 20-field set and provider link presence
Cluster detection and outlier handling; confidence gating on cluster coherence and subject similarity
Count reconciliation across selected set / sold count / valuation count / map count / registry count
Comp map and table share the same deduplicated records; links open safely in a new tab; raw LandPortal vs selected LandOS map differentiated
Valuation disclosure block
frontendScreens: Deal Card > Market > interactive comp map
Deal Card > Market > comp table and popups
workstreams.requiredDataState: A verified Deal Card with many sold comps whose selected top-five PPA is widely dispersed (acceptance example: 200 Sid Edens Rd — ~55 comps, PPA ~$6,250 to ~$64,516/acre).

steps: Open the Deal Card Market tab
Confirm each provider mission shows attempt/result and candidate/accepted/duplicate/rejected counts (no coverage claim without a result)
Open each selected comp popup and confirm the full validation field set including provider name and link (or an explicit missing-link marker)
Confirm the Realie.ai comp link works when available and all links open in a new tab safely
Confirm materially dispersed PPA clusters are not blended into one high-confidence median; confidence is downgraded with rationale
Confirm sold count, valuation count, map count, registry count, and selected set reconcile and map/table use the same deduplicated records
Refresh and confirm the comp set and valuation persist

testRequirements: Provider mission record carries attempt/result/blocker and the four counts; coverage requires a real result
Selected-comp validation requires the full field set; missing provider link is flagged not omitted
Cluster detection: dispersed PPA blocks high confidence; outliers explained
Count reconciliation invariant across selected/sold/valuation/map/registry
Map and table use identical deduplicated records
failureConditions: Provider coverage claimed with no real provider result
Selected comp missing a provider link with no visible marker; Realie.ai link broken when one is available
Five comps selected including weak/improved fillers when fewer are defensible
Dispersed PPA clusters blended into one high-confidence median; outliers unexplained
Displayed sold/valuation/map/registry counts disagree; map and table diverge

requirements.text: Every provider mission shows attempt/result plus candidate/accepted/duplicate/rejected counts and failure/blocker reason; coverage is not claimed because an adapter exists.
Every selected comp displays the full validation field set (address..why-it-outranked-next-best).
A missing provider URL is visibly identified rather than silently omitting view; the Realie.ai comp has a working link when available.
Weak comps are not selected to fill five; fewer than five are used when fewer are defensible; improved/unknown-improvement sales are excluded or penalized.
Materially different geographic/acreage/improvement/PPA clusters are detected and not blended into one high-confidence median; outliers are explained.
High confidence requires a coherent validated cluster and adequate subject similarity; otherwise preliminary/moderate/low/conflicted/insufficient.
Selected set, displayed sold count, valuation count, map count, and registry count reconcile; map and table use the same deduplicated records.
Provider links open safely in a new tab; LandPortal raw map and final LandOS selected map remain clearly differentiated.
Valuation discloses subject acreage basis, selected cluster, qualified closed-sale count, median/weighted/simple-average PPA, IQR, outliers, limitations, and confidence rationale.
No high-confidence valuation when access/zoning/property-type/acreage-basis/comp coherence materially undermines confidence.

workstreams.name: Executive audit and operator copy
workstreams.operatorOutcome: The consistency audit fails on each of the twelve enumerated inconsistencies and never reports all-pass while a visible inconsistency exists; misleading/awkward operator copy is repaired; seller questions are generated from actual unresolved facts and read naturally.
inScope: Extend the audit with the twelve new fail conditions
Audit reconciles frontend and backend readiness
Repair the enumerated copy defects (grammar, unsupported private-road, duplicate paragraphs, unhelpful jargon)
Seller-question generation from unresolved facts
workstreams.requiredDataState: A verified Deal Card exhibiting at least one of the twelve inconsistencies and the enumerated copy defects (acceptance example: 200 Sid Edens Rd).

steps: Open the Deal Card Overview executive audit
Confirm the audit does not report all-pass while readiness/acreage-basis/comp inconsistencies are visible
Seed or confirm each of the twelve fail conditions is detectable
Read operator copy and confirm the enumerated defects are repaired (grammar, private-road, duplicate paragraphs)
Open Seller and confirm questions come from real unresolved facts and read naturally
Refresh and confirm audit results and copy persist

testRequirements: Twelve audit fail-condition tests, each red on a seeded inconsistency and green when resolved
Copy tests: grammar of place phrase, single-component wording, no unsupported private-road, no duplicate paragraphs
Seller-question tests: generated from unresolved facts, no Unnamed-road-as-usable-access phrasing
failureConditions: Audit reports all-pass while a visible inconsistency exists
Any of the twelve conditions not detected
Enumerated copy defects still present
Seller questions reference Unnamed road as confirmed access or duplicate paragraphs remain

requirements.text: Audit fails on Strategy Readiness OK while all strategies blocked.
Audit fails on value confidence inconsistent with unresolved material facts.
Audit fails on missing Tyler decision for a material acreage discrepancy.
Audit fails on overlay acreage inconsistent with geometry acreage.
Audit fails on BFE claims unsupported by evidence.
Audit fails on partial access evidence counted as completed access research.
Audit fails on unknown road ownership described as private.
Audit fails on provider coverage claims without actual provider results.
Audit fails on a selected comp missing a provider link.
Audit fails on high-confidence valuation with incoherent comp clusters.
Audit fails on research-progress wording implying completed underwriting.
Audit fails on any frontend/backend readiness mismatch.
Enumerated misleading/awkward copy is repaired and seller questions are generated from actual unresolved facts and read naturally.

workstreams.name: Complete Deal Card browser regression
workstreams.operatorOutcome: The full Deal Card operator experience is inspected end to end across every tab and multiple fixtures (verified, incomplete, unresolved, APN-conflict, thin-market, multi-parcel) with frontend/API/DB/accepted-facts/ledger reconciliation, fresh screenshots, refresh and managed-restart persistence, and every finding repaired and retested.
inScope: Full click-through of Overview, Property, Due Diligence, Market, Strategy, Visuals, Seller, Documents, Activity
Interactive comp map, every selected comp popup, provider links, research status, readiness states, reports
Fixture coverage: acceptance example + two other jurisdictions + incomplete + unresolved + APN-conflict + thin-market + multi-parcel
Frontend/API/DB/accepted-facts/ledger reconciliation
Refresh and managed-restart persistence
frontendScreens: Deal Card > all tabs (Overview, Property, Due Diligence, Market, Strategy, Visuals, Seller, Documents, Activity)
workstreams.requiredDataState: The full fixture set: 200 Sid Edens Rd (APN 5105-00-44-0497) plus two verified properties in other jurisdictions, one incomplete-research, one unresolved, one APN-conflict, one thin-market, one multi-parcel.

steps: For each fixture, open the Deal Card and click through all nine tabs
Open the interactive comp map and every selected comp popup and follow provider links
Reconcile frontend against API payload, DB state, accepted operator facts, and the requirement ledger
Capture fresh screenshots for each tab and fixture
Refresh each card and confirm persistence
Run a managed restart (npm run landos:restart) and confirm persistence

testRequirements: Combined Deal Card regression suite green
System-consistency regression green across fixtures
Persistence checks (refresh + managed restart) recorded
failureConditions: Any tab shows an internally inconsistent or unqualified fact for any fixture
Frontend disagrees with API/DB/accepted facts/ledger
A finding is not repaired and retested
State does not persist across refresh or managed restart

requirements.text: Every Deal Card tab (Overview, Property, Due Diligence, Market, Strategy, Visuals, Seller, Documents, Activity) is inspected and internally consistent for every fixture.
Interactive comp map, every selected comp popup, provider links, research status, readiness states, and reports are inspected and reconciled.
Frontend reconciles with API payload, DB state, accepted operator facts, and the requirement ledger; accepted real-property information is not altered during QA.
Fixtures cover acceptance example, two other jurisdictions, incomplete-research, unresolved, APN-conflict, thin-market, and multi-parcel.
Fresh screenshots are captured; refresh and managed-restart persistence hold; every finding is repaired and retested.
